more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Chivayo battles to nullify marriage amid frozen assets and grounded jet in South Africa
more.description: Controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has filed an urgent High Court application seeking to throw out divorce proceedings brought by his estranged wife, Sonja Madzikanda, arguing their customary marriage ended in 2024. The legal dispute has expanded into South Africa, where assets linked to Chivayo have been frozen. The post Chivayo battles to nullify marriage amid frozen assets and grounded jet in South Africa appeared first on Nehanda Radio .

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Friendship Bench awarded EUR 250k KBF Africa Prize
more.description:
The King Baudouin Foundation awards 250,000 to Friendship Bench, a Zimbabwe- founded model partnering with governments and organisations to implement low- cost, fully integrated mental health care worldwide.

more.description: South Africa Warns Zimbabwe Over Stadium Readiness for Joint 2028 AFCON Bid South Africa’s Minister of Sport, Arts and Culture Gayton McKenzie has cast fresh doubt over Zimbabwe’s role in a proposed regional bid for the 2028 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON), saying participating countries must prove they have stadiums ready to meet CAF requirements. more.title: THE FINAL ACT: End of the road for Mnangagwa’s ally Chief Justice Luke Malaba... Rita Makarau is reportedly the next one
more.description: HARARE – As Chief Justice Luke Malaba prepares to bid farewell to the bench, the nation stands at a judicial crossroads. The Judicial Service Commission has confirmed that his last working day will be May 14, 2026, as he reaches the mandatory retirement age of 75 the following day. more.title: Shingai Shoniwa uses new single ‘Mhondoro’ to spotlight Zimbabwe’s rivers and cultural heritage
more.description: Musician Shingai Shoniwa has released a new single, “Mhondoro,” using music to raise awareness about environmental protection, river communities and Zimbabwean cultural identity. The song forms part of the global EarthSonic Flow project linking water stories from across the world. The post Shingai Shoniwa uses new single ‘Mhondoro’ to spotlight Zimbabwe’s rivers and cultural heritage appeared first on Nehanda Radio .
